metadata.name: Dog Ownership
metadata.description: Owning a dog is associated with reduced cardiovascular and all-cause mortality, likely through increased physical activity, enhanced social interactions, stress reduction, and companionship.

sources.citation: Kramer CK, Mehmood S, Suen RS. Dog Ownership and Survival: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis. Circulation: Cardiovascular Quality and Outcomes. 2019;12:e005554.

metadata.mechanismOfAction: Increased physical activity through dog walking, enhanced social interactions with other dog owners, stress reduction and companionship, improved mental health

applicability.notes: Most studies assessed sustained dog ownership (>1 year). Effect size based on meta-analysis of 10 studies with 3.8 million participants.
